Verified Petition For Preliminary Injunction And Order Compelling Respondents To Turn Over Or Account For Estate Property
Off Calendar Aug-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Kelly for Groovy Collectibles, Et Al. Robert Cross for Amelia Davis.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to this matter being heard/not being heard with the civil matter. Discussions regarding missing items, the Probate Court having jurisdiction to determine what the Estate owns, mediation, modification of the injunction, the Professional Service Agreement (PSA), etc were held. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial with the pending civil matter, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioner For Order Compelling Discovery; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Jul-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dan Kelly for Groovy Collectibles, et al. Robert Cross for and wih Amelia Davis (Moving Party).

Mr. Kelly stated that he had worked up a proposed order for items #6 & , addressed the deposition questions, and noted that there was a trial date set in the related matter for January 9, 2012. Mr. Cross stated that there was no contest as to item #11, that they will waive the deposition if the declaration covers..

The Court set status on the underlying petition filed 12/1 for August 8, 2011. In addition, the Court stated that the joint statements were due Tuesday, August 2, 2011, and that courtesy copies should be provided to Dept. 204. The Court then took the matter under submission with the intent of signing off that Counsel have stipulated except as to item #11.

Respondents' Motion For Order Modifying Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-29-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cross for and with Amelia Davis (Petitioner/Administrator). Daniel Kelly for Groovy Collectibles, LLC. Nick Despotopoulos and David Heureux also present.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions, stated that discovery still needs to be conducted and that they are not ready for trial. The Motion for Order Modifying Preliminary Injunction is denied. The Court denied Groovy's request to use the mailing list, the web site and any screen imaging stating that it was the Court's position that they belong to the Estate and that they were created because of the PSA (Professional Services Agreement). It is the Court's hope that Counsel communicate and come to an understanding. The Parties reached a stipulation with regard to the authentication language. The Court would like Counsel to work on an order reflecting today's ruling. If they can not agree, each Counsle is to submit their own proposed order. The hearing set for May 9, 2011 was continued to June 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. That date will be moved if a discovery motion is filed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Continued To Oct-18-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Geoffrey Adams for and with Arthur Andreas. Robert Cross for and with Amelia Davis. Daniel Kelly for Groovy Collectables.

Counsel reported that they agreed to terminate the 2010 contract between Groovy Collectibles and Mr. Andreas. Counsel also stated that they agreed to keep the petition for instructions on calendar and have Ms. Davis step into Mr. Andreas shoes and deal with the $190,000.00 creditor's claim. Mr. Cross stated that the objective was to have the settlement approved by the Court and that he filed the stipulation regarding valuation of the Estate. Mr. Cross is to file the stipulation that Mr. Kelly signed by October 18, 2010. Mr. Andreas is out as Administrator and Ms. Davis is in as Successor Administrator. The Petition for Instructions, the Petition to Cancel or Suspend Professional Services, and the Petition for Preliminary Injunction were continued to October 18, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement (subject to the written order).

Verified Petition Of Beneficiary Amelia Davis To Cancel Or Suspend Professional Services Agreement And Enjoin Sale Of Estate Assets
Off Calendar Aug-30-2010 Continued To Sep-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Geoffrey Adams for and with Arthur Andreas. Robert Cross for Amelia Davis. Daniel Kelly for Groovy Collectibles (Creditor/Interested Party).

The Court expressed concern about approving Attorney's/Executor's fees without having an Inventory and Appraisal of the assets, and without allowing the time period for the Creditor's Claims to run. Discussions were held regarding the disagreement as to the method of appraising the assets/photos, maintaining the status quo or transferring control over to Ms. Davis at this time, putting the Petition for Instructions filed on August 2, 2010 by Mr. Andreas back on calendar, the possibility of the Petition to Suspend Professional Services and the Petition for Preliminary Injunction going away if the Parties can stipulate that the 2010 contract with Groovy Collectibles is void/concluded, the need to dispose of the contents of the apartment, the division of fees, the determination as to the handling of the Groovy claim, and approving the settlement agreement with the exception of the Attorney's/Executor's fees and Mr. Kelly's concerns with regard to the Groovy contract. The matter was continued to September 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if they have a stipulation as to the value of the assets, an update on whether there are any other creditor's claims filed and a stipulation as to termination or conclusion of the 2010 Groovy Collectibles contract. The Petition for Instructions filed by Mr. Andreas is put back on calendar for September 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Verified Petition Of Beneficiary Amelia Davis To Cancel Or Suspend Professional Services Agreement And Enjoin Sale Of Estate Assets
Off Calendar Aug-16-2010 Continued To Aug-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Geoffrey Adams for and with Arthur Andreas. Robert Cross for and with Amelia Davis.

Mr. Cross stated that they had a global and comprehensive settlement between Mr. Andreas and Ms. Davis, and that Mr. Andreas will resign and will not be required to do an accounting. In addition, Mr. Cross stated that Christopher (Mr. Andreas' Son) declined to serve, that Ms. Davis would be appointed, that they filed the resignation, that the half Niece may need notice even though she was not named in the will, and requested shortening time on the Petition for Approval of the Settlement. Mr. Adams stated that the Niece received notice on the initial petition, that she did not act, requested the Court dispense with notice to the Niece and that he wanted the temporary restraining order to remain in place until the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement is heard. The Court asked Counsel to sign/file a stipulation with regard to the temporary restraining order remaining in place. The Court stated that notice to the Niece was not required as Ms. Davis was the sole Beneficiary. The Petition for Instructions on calendar for September 1, 2010 is off calendar. The hearing on the Petition for Instructions is advanced from September 21, 2010 to August 30, 2010. Mr. Cross is to submit the order advancing the petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Ex Parte Application By Petitioner For Order Limiting Personal Representative'S Powers, And For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Jun-24-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cross for Amelia Davis (Sole Beneficiary). Geoffrey Adams for and with Arthur Andreas (Executor). Daniel Kelly for Groovy Collectibles.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to validity of the contract entered between the Executor and Groovy Collectibles, the inventory not being completed, the Beneficiary not being included in the inventory process, the manner in which the business is being/was operated and potential loss to the Estate and the Sole Beneficiary. The Court granted the temporary restraining order (signing the order for the same in open court). The hearing on the preliminary injucntion was continued to August 16, 2010 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. The moving papers are to be served by end of business tomorrow (June 25, 2010). Oposition papers are due August 2, 2010. Replies are due by August 9, 2010. No action is to be taken such as marketing, offering or selling of any photographic prints or related assets of the Estate under the Professional Services Agreement (PSA) dated May 6, 2010 between the Personal Representative and Groovy Collecitbles

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
